---
title: "06f-gene-expression.Rmd"
output: html_document
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
```{r}
# importing the dataset 
require(tidyverse)
salmon.data <- read.delim("C:/Users/ashra/Downloads/salmon.isoform.TPM.not_cross_norm", header=FALSE)
```
```{r}
#filtering for only 25 and 5 treatments
salmon.data$V1 = NULL
salmon.data$V2 = NULL
salmon.data$V3 = NULL
salmon.data$V4 = NULL
salmon.data$V5 = NULL
salmon.data$V6 = NULL
salmon.data$V7 = NULL
```
```{r}
#importing the other dataset 
Sequencing.Picks <- read.csv("C:/Users/ashra/Downloads/2023-crab-genotype - Sequencing Picks.csv")
```
```{r}
#importing final dataset
metadata.main <- read.csv("C:/Users/ashra/Downloads/metabolomics-sample-list.csv")
```
